text.title: EVP Five-Pillar Framework
text.summary: The five EVP pillars, Discover-Define-Deliver-Measure process, and Gartner Human Deal evolution.

rule.statement: Structure every EVP around exactly five pillars: Compensation, Benefits, Career, Work Environment, and Culture/Purpose. Each pillar must have at least three named proof points before external use.
rule.rationale: A pillar without proof points is aspirational copy. Glassdoor reviews immediately expose claims that do not match employee experience, creating recruiter credibility loss.

list.item: Compensation: base salary, bonuses, equity, perks — total financial rewards.
Benefits: health, PTO, parental leave, wellness — non-financial rewards.
Career: promotions, learning budget, mentorship, mobility — growth opportunities.
Work Environment: flexibility, tools, team culture, office — day-to-day experience.
Culture/Purpose: mission, values, impact, belonging — why the work matters.

rule.statement: Follow the Discover-Define-Deliver-Measure sequence. Do not skip Discover (surveys + exit interviews + competitor analysis) before drafting the EVP statement.
rule.rationale: EVP drafted without Discover produces leadership talking points, not what employees actually experience. The gap between aspiration and reality is the leading cause of EVP backlash.

list.item: Discover: employee surveys, exit interviews, candidate feedback, competitor analysis.
Define: identify unique strengths, prioritize top 3-5 attributes, craft EVP statement, develop pillars.
Deliver: communicate internally first, integrate into employer brand, train recruiters, embed in candidate experience.
Measure: track application rate, offer acceptance, referrals, Glassdoor rating, candidate NPS.

section.title: Gartner Human Deal evolution
section.p: Gartner research (2021+) shows the traditional transactional EVP ("what we offer") is evolving toward a Human Deal that addresses emotional needs, not just contractual ones.

list.item: Deeper Connections: family and community support so employees feel understood.
Radical Flexibility: autonomy over when, where, and how work happens.
Personal Growth: grow as people, not just professionals.
Holistic Well-being: physical, mental, financial, and social wellness.
